title: How Trains Drove American History

segments.title: What Changed
segments.audioText: The transcontinental railroad changed just about everything in America: transportation, communications, commerce, cities, politics, even our perception of time.
segments.transcript: The transcontinental railroad changed just about everything in America: transportation, communications, commerce, cities, politics, even our perception of time.
segments.translationZh: 橫貫大陸鐵路幾乎改變咗美國所有嘢：交通、通訊、商業、城市、政治，甚至我哋對時間嘅理解。

points.label: changed just about everything
points.explanationZh: just about everything 即『幾乎所有嘢』，聽到 just about 要當成一個柔和但範圍好大嘅講法。

points.label: transportation, communications, commerce
points.explanationZh: 呢串名詞係新聞常見列表，先抓每個大類，唔使逐字緊張。

points.label: perception of time
points.explanationZh: perception 係『理解／看法』；of time 表示對時間本身嘅觀念。

segments.title: At Steamtown
segments.audioText: I'm David Pogue at the Steamtown National Historic Site in Pennsylvania.
segments.transcript: I'm David Pogue at the Steamtown National Historic Site in Pennsylvania.
segments.translationZh: 我係 David Pogue，而家喺賓夕凡尼亞州嘅 Steamtown National Historic Site。

points.label: I'm David Pogue
points.explanationZh: 記者自我介紹好短，I'm + name，聽新聞片頭好常見。

points.label: at the Steamtown National Historic Site
points.explanationZh: at 後面接具體地點；National Historic Site 即國家歷史景點。

points.label: in Pennsylvania
points.explanationZh: in 加州名，補充地點位置。美國新聞常用『地點 + in + 州名』。

segments.title: Big Boy
segments.audioText: And this is Big Boy, the biggest functioning steam train in the world.
segments.transcript: And this is Big Boy, the biggest functioning steam train in the world.
segments.translationZh: 而呢架就係 Big Boy，世界上最大而仍然可以運作嘅蒸汽火車。

points.label: And this is
points.explanationZh: And this is... 係介紹眼前物件嘅自然口語開場。

points.label: the biggest functioning steam train
points.explanationZh: biggest 係最高級；functioning 即仲運作緊，steam train 即蒸汽火車。

points.label: in the world
points.explanationZh: in the world 放句尾，加強『全世界』嘅範圍。

sentences: The transcontinental railroad changed just about everything in America: transportation, communications, commerce, cities, politics, even our perception of time.
I'm David Pogue at the Steamtown National Historic Site in Pennsylvania.
And this is Big Boy, the biggest functioning steam train in the world.

vocabulary.example: The transcontinental railroad connected distant parts of the country.
vocabulary.translationZh: 橫貫大陸鐵路連接咗國家入面相距好遠嘅地方。

vocabulary.example: The railroad changed travel and trade.
vocabulary.translationZh: 鐵路改變咗旅行同貿易。

vocabulary.example: Transportation became faster after the railroad expanded.
vocabulary.translationZh: 鐵路擴展之後，交通變得更快。
更好嘅通訊幫助城市發展。

vocabulary.example: Railroads helped commerce move across the country.
vocabulary.translationZh: 鐵路幫助商業活動喺全國流動。

vocabulary.example: Technology can change our perception of time.
vocabulary.translationZh: 科技可以改變我哋對時間嘅看法。

vocabulary.example: The museum keeps a functioning steam train.
vocabulary.translationZh: 博物館保留咗一架仲運作緊嘅蒸汽火車。

phrases.example: The new schedule changed just about everything.

phrases.meaningZh: 我哋對時間嘅理解／感覺
phrases.example: Remote work changed our perception of time.
We visited a National Historic Site during the trip.
It is one of the oldest stations in the world.

speakingPractice.promptZh: 由 00:00 開始跟讀第一句，先練 changed just about everything，再讀成串名詞列表。
重播 00:12，聽寫 I'm David Pogue at the Steamtown National Historic Site in Pennsylvania.，再對稿。
用 And this is... 練介紹物件：And this is the oldest train in the museum.
用兩句英文講返重點：The railroad changed many parts of American life. Big Boy is a huge working steam train.

notes: 今日片段係 CBS Sunday Morning 約 3 分 25 秒嘅歷史文化新聞短片，主題係鐵路點樣影響美國發展。
聽力重點係列表句：transportation、communications、commerce、cities、politics。先聽大方向，再補細節。
重播時間碼時，留意記者用 at + 地點、in + 州名去快速交代位置。
dailyLifePractice 會配合『交通／出行』主題，練問路、確認班次、同解釋小延誤。

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking Which Train to Take
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你喺車站想確認要搭邊班車，講法要清楚又唔太正式。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Excuse me, which train goes downtown? Take the blue line from this platform. Does it stop at Central Station? Yes, it does.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Excuse me, which train goes downtown?
lines.zh: 唔好意思，邊班車去市中心？

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.en: Take the blue line from this platform.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Does it stop at Central Station?

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.en: Yes, it does. It's about four stops away.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: which train goes... 好自然直接，適合車站問人。Does it stop at...? 係確認站名最實用嘅問法。

drills.label: substitution
drills.prompt: 用 which train goes... 換三個目的地。
drills.example: Which train goes to the airport?

drills.label: quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 Does it stop at Central Station? 三次，尾音保持上揚。
drills.example: Does it stop at Central Station?

dailyLifePractice.title: Checking a Delay
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你見到交通有延誤，想問清楚大約要等幾耐。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Is this train running late? Yeah, about ten minutes. Thanks for letting me know. No problem.

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.en: No problem. It should be here soon.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: running late 係交通同日程延誤常用講法，比 delayed 更口語。Thanks for letting me know 自然又有禮貌。

drills.label: short response practice
drills.prompt: 聽到 about ten minutes，用一句英文回應。

drills.label: say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 running late 講巴士、會議或朋友遲到。
drills.example: The bus is running late today.

dailyLifePractice.title: Explaining Your Route
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你同朋友講自己點樣過去，簡短交代交通安排。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: I'm taking the train in. I'll switch lines at Union Station. I should get there around three. Text me when you're close.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Do you need a ride from the station?
lines.zh: 你需唔需要我喺車站接你？

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I’ll switch lines at Union Station.
lines.zh: 我會喺 Union Station 轉線。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I should get there around three.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: I'm taking the train in 入面嘅 in 係『入城／入去嗰邊』嘅感覺，好美式。I should get there around... 係預計到達時間，聽落唔死板。

drills.label: role-play
drills.prompt: 你做 You，講自己會點樣去同幾點到。
drills.example: I'm taking the train in. I should get there around five.

drills.label: substitution
drills.prompt: 用 I'll switch lines at... 換成其他站名。
